etymology.description: 不(bù) (bù, "not") negates the verb phrase 知道(知道) (zhīdào, "to know"). Together, they express the concept of lacking knowledge.
etymology.interpretation: 不(bù, "not") directly negates the affirmative state described by 知道(知道) (zhīdào, "to know"). When you are not in a state of knowing, you simply do not have that knowledge, leading to 'don't know'.

parts.english: to know; be aware of; be acquainted with

examples.chinese: 我不知道今天有什么计划(我不知道今天有什麼計劃)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ bù zhīdào jīntiān yǒu shénme jìhuà.
examples.english: I don't know what the plan is for today.

examples.chinese: 他不知道问题的答案(他不知道問題的答案)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bù zhīdào wèntí de dá'àn.
examples.english: He doesn't know the answer to the question.

examples.chinese: 她不知道明天是否下雨(她不知道明天是否下雨)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bù zhīdào míngtiān shìfǒu xià yǔ.
examples.english: She doesn't know if it will rain tomorrow.
